projects.title: Urban Logic — Real Estate Board Package Platform
projects.content:
Urban Logic is a full-stack real estate application management platform built for NYC co-op and rental buildings. The system handles the complete lifecycle of board package applications — from multi-step form completion and deal party management, to Stripe payment processing, PandaDoc e-signature workflows, OneApp credit and background screening, and board approval.

The platform serves multiple user roles: applicants, co-applicants, guarantors, brokers, property managers, board members, and customer support staff. Each role has a distinct workflow with role-based access controls, conditional form logic, and audit trail logging for every action taken.

I contributed across the full stack — building and hardening the Laravel 12 REST API backend (PHP 8.2+), implementing third-party service integrations, and working on the React 18 SPA frontend. Work included a comprehensive security and performance audit covering input validation, webhook signature verification, race condition prevention, cascade delete optimization, and elimination of code vulnerabilities including an eval()-based RCE risk in the fee calculation engine.

The platform is live in production, serving real property management companies and applicants across New York City.
projects.summary: A USA-based full-stack real estate board package platform for NYC properties, featuring Stripe payment processing, PandaDoc e-signatures, OneApp credit screening, AWS S3 document storage, and multi-role application management for co-op and rental buildings.
projects.problem: NYC property management companies needed a centralised digital platform to manage the complex board package application process — a multi-party, document-heavy workflow involving applicants, brokers, guarantors, property managers, and board members. Existing processes relied on paper forms, email chains, and manual document collection, making it slow, error-prone, and difficult to track at scale.
projects.solution: Built a comprehensive full-stack platform that digitises the entire application lifecycle. Applicants fill multi-step conditional forms, pay fees via Stripe, sign documents via PandaDoc e-signatures, and submit to board — all within a single platform. Property managers oversee applications, trigger credit/background checks via OneApp, manage refunds, and send packages to the board. Board members review and approve or reject directly in the system. Every action is logged to a full audit trail.
features: Multi-step conditional form engine with per-building, per-role, per-application-type workflows
Stripe payment gateway integration — invoices, tax rates (NY/NJ), refunds, and webhook handling
PandaDoc e-signature integration — document generation, token mapping, embedded signing, webhook lifecycle
OneApp credit and background screening — screening initiation, PDF retrieval, and webhook processing
AWS S3 document storage with pre-signed URL generation for secure file access
Role-based access control with 7 user roles: Applicant, Co-Applicant, Guarantor, Applicant Broker, Owner Broker, Property Manager, Board Member
Role-switching — brokers and property managers can act on behalf of deal parties
Full audit trail logging every user action to a UserActivity table with timestamps and IP addresses
SendGrid transactional email notifications for invitations, board decisions, and application status changes
Real-time broadcasting via Pusher for live application status updates
Application completion percentage tracking via async background jobs
Board package PDF generation and financial summary document upload to PandaDoc
highlights: End-to-end Stripe integration handling invoices, taxes, refunds, and idempotent webhook processing
PandaDoc document lifecycle — generation, section upload, embedded signing, and signed document archival to S3
Conducted and implemented a comprehensive 48-item security, bug, performance, and code quality audit
Replaced an eval()-based RCE vulnerability in the fee calculation engine with a safe recursive descent arithmetic parser
Implemented HMAC-SHA256 webhook signature verification for both PandaDoc and Stripe webhooks
Optimised Application cascade soft-delete from N+1 in-memory collection operations to batch SQL UPDATE queries
Multi-role conditional form system supporting building-specific, application-type-specific workflows
challenges: Coordinating a multi-party signing workflow across PandaDoc, AWS S3, and the application database with reliable state tracking
Building a conditional form engine where visible sections, required fields, and dependent worksheets vary per building, role, and application type
Securing webhooks from Stripe, PandaDoc, and OneApp with proper signature verification and environment filtering
Preventing duplicate payment records under concurrent requests using pessimistic DB locking
Managing a self-referencing ResponseWorksheet tree for nested conditional form sections
Supporting role-switching (brokers acting as applicants) across the full API and frontend state layers
impact: Digitised the entire NYC board package application process for multiple live property buildings
Reduced manual document handling and email coordination for property management companies
Enabled applicants, brokers, and property managers to collaborate in a single tracked platform
Secured payment and document workflows processing real transactions and legally-binding e-signatures
Hardened production system through a full security audit — eliminating RCE risks, auth gaps, and data exposure vulnerabilities
learnings: Designing multi-tenant, multi-role access control systems with granular per-resource authorization
Integrating and hardening multiple third-party payment and document APIs in a production environment
Identifying and fixing subtle bugs — race conditions, null pointer paths, and incorrect boolean logic — through systematic auditing
Optimising database-heavy operations (cascade deletes, N+1 queries) for production scale
Building conditional form workflows driven entirely by database configuration rather than hardcoded logic

projects.title: Express Trackers — Logistics & Freight Forwarding Website
projects.content:
Express Trackers is a professionally designed multi-page business website built for a Mumbai-based logistics and freight forwarding company operating since 2014. The site was built from scratch using pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript — no frameworks, no dependencies.

The website covers the company's full range of services including Ocean Freight, Air Freight, Custom Clearance, Courier & Cargo, Road Transportation, Warehousing, and Marine Cargo Insurance. It presents the brand's key differentiators — real-time Web & SMS shipment tracking, transparent pricing, and 12+ years of experience — to its target audience of small and medium-sized businesses.

Design focus was placed on building a credibility-first experience: clean layout, clear service breakdowns, an animated stat counter section, a 4-step process flow, and a fully functional contact section with WhatsApp integration. The site is fully responsive across desktop and mobile devices.
projects.summary: A multi-page business website for an India-based logistics and freight forwarding company, built with pure HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Covers 7 freight services, real-time shipment tracking, and SMB-focused positioning for a company with 12+ years of experience and 15,000+ parcels delivered.
projects.problem: Express Trackers, a founder-led logistics company based in Vasai (Mumbai), lacked a professional web presence that could communicate their full service range and decade-long credibility to potential SMB clients. Without a structured site, converting inquiries and establishing trust with new customers was difficult.
projects.solution: Designed and developed a clean, multi-page static website that clearly presents all 7 logistics services, showcases key business metrics (12+ years, 15,000+ parcels delivered, 98% satisfaction rate), and guides visitors through a simple inquiry-to-delivery process flow. The site includes a WhatsApp contact shortcut, social media links, and a full contact page to make reaching the team frictionless.
features: Multi-page layout — Home, About, Services, and Contact pages
Animated business metrics: 12+ years experience, 15,000+ parcels, 26+ clients, 98% satisfaction
7-service breakdown with individual descriptions: Ocean Freight, Air Freight, Custom Clearance, Courier & Cargo, Road Transportation, Warehousing, and Marine Cargo Insurance
4-step process flow: Inquiry → Planning → Shipping → Delivery
WhatsApp direct contact integration
Fully responsive layout for desktop and mobile
Company mission, vision, and core values section
Subsidiary division (Dhruv Transport) highlighted in road logistics section
highlights: Built entirely with vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript — zero frameworks or dependencies
Credibility-first design presenting 12+ years of logistics expertise to SMB clients
Structured service pages that clearly differentiate 7 distinct freight and logistics offerings
Clean, professional branding aligned with the company's trust and reliability positioning
challenges: Translating a service-heavy business with 7 distinct offerings into a clear, non-overwhelming layout
Building responsive behaviour from scratch without a CSS framework
Structuring content to appeal to SMB decision-makers who need quick answers on capabilities and pricing
Presenting a founder-led business with professional credibility on par with larger logistics firms
impact: Gave Express Trackers a professional online presence representing 12+ years of logistics operations
Improved discoverability and credibility for SMB clients seeking freight forwarding services in India
Streamlined the inquiry process with direct WhatsApp and contact form access
Presented all 7 services in a structured format, reducing back-and-forth for potential clients
learnings: Designing information-dense business sites with clear visual hierarchy
Writing semantic, accessible HTML without relying on frameworks
Structuring multi-service content for fast comprehension by non-technical business audiences
Building fully responsive layouts with pure CSS across device sizes

projects.title: Hanusan Pharmaceutical — D2C Nutraceutical E-Commerce Store
projects.content:
Hanusan Pharmaceutical is a direct-to-consumer nutraceutical e-commerce brand founded by pharmacist Nitesh Roshan, based in Vasai West, Maharashtra, India. The brand is built on a preventive healthcare philosophy — 'Prevention is Powerful. Quality is Non-Negotiable.' — and positions its products on pharmacist-led credibility, clinical backing, and transparent formulations.

The store was built on Shopify with a custom storefront design using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The work involved building a conversion-optimised product experience for their flagship supplement San-D (Vitamin D3 + K2), including a comparison section, certification display, FAQ, testimonials, and promotional offer mechanics.

The site is fully e-commerce ready — with multi-gateway payment support (UPI, cards, Google Pay, Apple Pay, PayPal, net banking), discount/coupon functionality, a customer review system, and India-wide shipping. Certifications displayed include GMP, FSSAI, ISO, FDA (India), Vegan, and Made in India.
projects.summary: An India-based D2C nutraceutical e-commerce store for a pharmacist-founded supplement brand, built on Shopify with custom HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Features multi-gateway payments (UPI, cards, Google Pay, Apple Pay, PayPal), promo code support, product comparison UI, and certification-led credibility design.
projects.problem: A pharmacist-founded nutraceutical brand needed a credibility-first e-commerce storefront that could communicate clinical differentiation — not just list a product. Generic Shopify themes don't convey scientific trust, certifications, or ingredient-level comparisons in a way that converts health-conscious buyers who are sceptical of supplement brands.
projects.solution: Built a custom Shopify storefront with a conversion-focused design that leads with clinical credibility — pharmacist founding story, GMP/FSSAI/ISO/FDA certifications, a direct comparison table against D3-only competitors, customer testimonials, and a structured FAQ addressing medical concerns. Multi-gateway checkout (UPI, cards, digital wallets, PayPal) ensures frictionless purchase across India's diverse payment preferences.
features: Custom Shopify storefront design with clinical, credibility-first layout
Product comparison table — San-D D3+K2 vs generic D3-only supplements (highlighting hypercalcemia risk)
Certification display section — GMP, FSSAI, ISO, FDA (India), Vegan, Made in India
Multi-gateway payment support — UPI, Visa, Mastercard, Maestro, Amex, Google Pay, Apple Pay, PayPal, net banking
Promotional pricing mechanics — prepaid discount (₹100 off), first-order promo code (SAND), free shipping threshold
Customer review and star rating system — 19 reviews, 4.5/5 aggregate
FAQ section addressing clinical usage, medication compatibility, pregnancy safety, and long-term use
Founder story and pharmacist credibility section on About page
Mobile-responsive layout optimised for smartphone shoppers
Cart, account creation, login, and full checkout flow
highlights: Conversion-optimised design for a science-backed health brand launching its first product
Clinical comparison table distinguishing D3+K2 synergy from inferior D3-only alternatives
Full multi-gateway payment integration covering all major Indian and international payment methods
Certification trust strip (GMP, FSSAI, ISO, FDA, Vegan) built to convert sceptical supplement buyers
Achieved 4.5/5 star rating from early users with the flagship product selling out post-launch
challenges: Designing a supplement storefront that feels clinical and trustworthy without overstating medical claims (regulatory compliance in copy)
Differentiating a single-product launch in a crowded Indian nutraceutical market through design and messaging alone
Building a comparison UI that is persuasive without being misleading or triggering regulatory issues
Optimising checkout conversion across India's fragmented payment landscape (UPI-first users, card users, wallet users)
Communicating complex nutritional science (D3+K2 synergy, MK-7 bioavailability) in accessible, non-jargon language
impact: Launched a pharmacist-founded D2C supplement brand with a professional, credibility-first online presence
Flagship product San-D sold out post-launch with 19 customer reviews and a 4.5/5 satisfaction rating
Enabled India-wide sales across diverse payment methods — UPI, cards, wallets, and net banking
Helped position a new brand against established supplement competitors through ingredient-level comparison design
Provided a scalable Shopify foundation ready for new product additions (dermaceutical pipeline)
learnings: Designing health and wellness e-commerce with regulatory awareness — balancing persuasion and compliance in copy
Building Shopify storefronts with custom HTML, CSS, and JavaScript beyond default theme capabilities
Structuring product pages for clinical credibility — certifications, comparisons, FAQs, and founder trust signals
Optimising for India's payment diversity — UPI, digital wallets, cards, and net banking in a single checkout flow
Translating a founder's domain expertise (pharmacist) into a brand story that converts first-time supplement buyers

projects.title: Energy Transition Platform — South Asia Clean Energy Data Hub
projects.content:
The Energy Transition Platform (ETP) South Asia is a Heinrich Böll Stiftung-funded, multi-country collaborative hub founded in 2018 to accelerate clean energy transition across eight South Asian nations — Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka. The secretariat is hosted by Vasudha Foundation, New Delhi.

The platform serves policymakers, energy researchers, civil society organisations, and development agencies across the region. It provides structured country energy data, NDC (Nationally Determined Contributions) tracking, interactive energy scenario dashboards, climate finance tracking, and a 35+ article knowledge library — all maintained and developed by VistaarDigital.

The website is built on WordPress with custom-developed interactive data dashboards for the Energy Scenario tool, Air Quality Index visualiser, and Climate Finance tracker. Country profile pages follow a standardised data template covering installed capacity, generation trends, electrification rates, EV landscapes, GHG emissions, and expert interviews — built and maintained across all eight nations with multi-year data from 2016 to 2025.
projects.summary: A multi-country South Asian clean energy data and policy platform built and maintained for a Heinrich Böll Stiftung-funded NGO consortium. Features interactive energy scenario dashboards, NDC country portals, AQI tracking, and climate finance data across 8 nations — built on WordPress with custom data visualisation tools.
projects.problem: Eight South Asian nations — with vastly different energy profiles, regulatory frameworks, and geopolitical relationships — lacked a shared, neutral platform to track clean energy progress, compare NDC commitments, and exchange knowledge. Policymakers and civil society had no centralised data source to measure regional energy transition trends or identify cross-border cooperation opportunities.
projects.solution: Built and maintained a comprehensive multi-country web platform providing standardised country energy profiles, interactive dashboards tracking fossil vs. non-fossil capacity from 2016–2025, NDC portals for all eight nations, AQI historical data, and a climate finance tracker. The platform normalises inconsistent national data formats (different fiscal years across India, Pakistan, Bhutan, and Afghanistan) into comparable calendar-year datasets for regional analysis.
features: Interactive Energy Scenario Dashboard — multi-year (2016–2025) installed capacity, generation, and per-capita data for all 8 South Asian countries with fossil vs. non-fossil mix visualisation
NDC Country Portal — Nationally Determined Contributions for all 8 nations (submission years 2016–2022) with structured country sub-pages
Air Quality Index Dashboard — historical AQI visualisation from 2021–2025 across South Asian cities
Climate Finance Tracker — GCF (Green Climate Fund) and GEF flows across the region
8 standardised Country Energy Profiles — installed capacity, generation trends, electrification rates, coal plant data, GHG emissions, and sectoral demand breakdowns
Electric Vehicles sections for Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka
Expert Interview series — embedded per-country video/text interviews with in-country energy specialists
35+ article knowledge hub covering cross-border trade, green hydrogen, sustainable finance, gender in energy, and national energy scenarios
Events system — annual partner meetings, webinars, and regional consultations
Data methodology documentation — calendar-year normalisation of varying national fiscal year formats
highlights: Data platform covering 8 South Asian nations with 10 years of standardised energy statistics (2016–2025)
Built for a Heinrich Böll Stiftung-funded NGO consortium with Vasudha Foundation, Grameen Shakti, Practical Action, and 8 other partner organisations
Interactive dashboards tracking a 10.71% regional shift from fossil to non-fossil fuel capacity over the platform's lifetime
NDC portal centralising climate commitments across Afghanistan, Bangladesh, Bhutan, India, Maldives, Nepal, Pakistan, and Sri Lanka
Sole digital infrastructure provider (VistaarDigital) for a multi-country policy platform used by policymakers, researchers, and development agencies
challenges: Normalising energy data from 8 countries with different fiscal year calendars (India: April–March; Pakistan: July–June; Bhutan: January–August) into comparable calendar-year datasets
Building standardised country profile templates flexible enough to reflect vastly different energy contexts — from Bhutan (99.64% renewable) to Bangladesh (4.61% renewable)
Maintaining data accuracy across fragmented national sources: NEA annual reports, NEPRA reports, UNFCCC BURs, national ministry roadmaps, and biennial transparency reports
Designing accessible dashboards for a diverse audience spanning technical researchers, civil society advocates, and non-technical policymakers across 8 countries
Keeping country data current for Afghanistan post-2021 given severe limitations on available government data after the political transition
impact: Provides the only unified, publicly accessible energy data repository spanning all 8 South Asian nations in a standardised, comparable format
Tracks and communicates a 10.71% regional clean energy shift — giving policymakers and donors measurable evidence of transition progress
Hosts 35+ research articles and policy briefs informing energy transition decisions across the region
Convened 4 major partner meetings (Bangkok 2018, Bangkok 2019, Colombo 2023, Kathmandu 2024) and multiple webinars with civil society, policymakers, and development agencies
Supported a consortium of 11 partner organisations across 8 countries in sharing knowledge and coordinating regional clean energy advocacy
learnings: Building data platforms that must reconcile inconsistent, multi-source national datasets into coherent regional visualisations
Designing policy-sector websites where credibility, data accuracy, and neutral presentation are more important than conversion or engagement metrics
Managing long-term content maintenance across 8 country profiles, each with independent data sources and update cycles
Developing interactive dashboards within a WordPress ecosystem using custom JavaScript and data visualisation libraries
Working with NGO and development sector clients where stakeholder alignment across multiple partner organisations shapes every content and design decision

projects.title: ICED — India Climate & Energy Dashboard (NITI Aayog)
projects.content:
The India Climate & Energy Dashboard (ICED) is a flagship data visualisation and analytics platform developed by Vistaar Digital for NITI Aayog — India's apex public policy think tank under the Government of India. The platform is hosted on NIC (National Informatics Centre) infrastructure and built in partnership with Vasudha Foundation as the knowledge partner.

ICED serves as India's centralised, publicly accessible repository for climate, energy, electricity, environment, economy, and demographic data — covering all 28 states and 8 union territories. It bridges data from dozens of government ministries and departments (Ministry of Coal, Ministry of Power, MNRE, CEA, MoSPI, RBI, Central Water Commission, and more) into a single, interactive, filterable dashboard with chart and Excel data downloads.

The platform directly tracks India's NDC commitments — including the 45% emission intensity reduction target (50% achieved as of the latest data) and the Net Zero 2070 goal — while aggregating 23 external government energy portals into a meta-portal gateway for India's broader energy data ecosystem.
projects.summary: A Government of India flagship data platform built for NITI Aayog, covering India's climate, energy, electricity, environment, and economic data across all 28 states. Features interactive dashboards, geospatial maps, NDC tracking, DISCOM financial analytics, and 23 aggregated government portals — developed and maintained by Vistaar Digital.
projects.problem: India's climate and energy data was fragmented across dozens of government ministries, departments, and portals — inaccessible to policymakers, researchers, and state governments in a unified, comparable format. With India's NDC commitments, Net Zero 2070 goal, and G20 climate obligations requiring evidence-based decision-making, there was no single platform to track energy transition progress, GHG emissions, DISCOM performance, and economic indicators together.
projects.solution: Built a comprehensive, cross-domain data dashboard that aggregates official data from the Ministry of Coal, Ministry of Power, MNRE, CEA, RBI, MoSPI, and more into a single interactive platform. The platform provides filterable time-series visualisations, state-level drill-downs, geospatial maps, nine dedicated analytics tools, and direct XLS data downloads — while also acting as a gateway to 23 external government energy portals.
features: Energy module — coal, oil, gas, solar, wind, hydro, nuclear, biofuels with reserves, production, consumption, pricing, and pipeline data
Electricity module — installed capacity mix, plant-level database, generation trends, PLF/CUF, forced/planned outages, CO₂ from generation, and pipeline capacity by fuel type
DISCOM analytics — AT&C losses, T&D losses, billing/collection efficiency, accumulated deficits, cost of supply, tariff subsidy gap, regulatory assets
RPO (Renewable Purchase Obligation) compliance tracking by state
GHG emissions tracking — economy-wide, energy sector, IPPU, LULUCF, agriculture, and waste
Climate variability — temperature trends and rainfall patterns over time
State Reports — comprehensive single-page profiles for all 28 states and 8 UTs combining energy, climate, economy, and demographic data
Nine dedicated analytics tools — including RE installed vs potential gap, EV transition tracker, DISCOM financial health, peak demand vs temperature correlation, and crude oil import analysis
Interactive geospatial maps using Leaflet.js — air quality, forest area, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks, land cover, water resources
NDC progress tracker — India's 45% emission intensity reduction target with live progress indicator
23 external government portals aggregated — VEDAS, PRAAPTI, SAUBHAGYA, UDAY, TARANG, E-Amrit, VAHAN, BHUVAN, India WRIS, and more
Data download in JPEG (charts) and XLS (raw data) formats across all sections
LIFE Initiative section — tracking India's 1 Billion Pro Planet People goal (2022–2028)
highlights: Built for NITI Aayog — India's apex government policy institution — hosted on NIC infrastructure under the gov.in domain
One of India's most comprehensive publicly accessible energy and climate data platforms spanning 7 major domains
State-level granularity across all 28 Indian states and 8 union territories with year and month-based filtering
Plant-level electricity database including heat rates, FGD status, water sources, and ramping parameters for individual power plants
DISCOM financial health tracker providing rare public visibility into distribution company losses and regulatory assets
Aggregates data from 10+ government ministries into a single filterable, downloadable dashboard
challenges: Aggregating data from 10+ government ministries with inconsistent formats, update cycles, and granularity levels into a unified, coherent platform
Building interactive, performant dashboards for deeply nested multi-dimensional data (state × fuel type × year × metric) within a government infrastructure environment
Designing accessible data visualisations for a diverse audience spanning technical researchers, civil society, and non-technical policymakers
Maintaining data accuracy across assumed and derived figures while clearly documenting methodology and disclaimers
Integrating Leaflet.js geospatial maps with government data layers (air quality, forest cover, national parks, water resources) at national scale
Operating within NIC hosting constraints and gov.in domain requirements while delivering a modern, SPA-style frontend experience
impact: Provides the Government of India and public researchers a single platform to track NDC progress — India's 45% emission intensity target now 50% achieved, visible in real time
Enables state governments and policymakers to benchmark renewable energy deployment against potential capacity and identify transition gaps
Brings rare public transparency to DISCOM financial performance — AT&C losses, accumulated deficits, and cost-revenue gaps — critical for India's power sector reform
Aggregates 23 previously siloed government energy portals into a single discovery and navigation hub
Supports India's Net Zero 2070 and G20 energy commitments with a credible, government-backed data infrastructure
learnings: Architecting large-scale government data platforms with multi-domain, multi-source data requiring rigorous methodology documentation
Building performant React-based dashboards with deep state/time filtering across hundreds of data series
Integrating Leaflet.js geospatial maps with national-scale government datasets
Navigating government project constraints — NIC hosting, gov.in domain, multi-stakeholder sign-off — while delivering a modern frontend
Designing data visualisations that serve both technical analysts and high-level policy audiences from a single platform
